skill.name: chronicle-assistant-guide
skill.description: Guidance for AI assistants using Chronicle to search session history, inspect roadmaps, and choose MCP or CLI workflows.
skill.summary: Helps AI assistants use Chronicle history before starting work.
skill.author: ChandlerHardy

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many Ruby backtick, sensitive file, and blocker patterns, but the reviewed file is Markdown guidance and examples. No malicious execution, prompt injection, network exfiltration, or credential theft was found. The remaining risk is that the guide encourages broad Chronicle history searches and local CLI use, which may expose local assistant history if not scoped.

medium_findings.title: Broad Chronicle History Search May Expose Local Context
medium_findings.description: The guide tells assistants to search Chronicle before work and applies across all projects. This is useful, but searches may reveal prior sessions, summaries, or project history if users do not scope queries.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly directs assistants to search Chronicle history and documents the local session database and transcript locations. The behavior is legitimate, but the privacy exposure is clear.

medium_findings.title: Assistant-Directed Local CLI Commands
medium_findings.description: The skill includes many Chronicle CLI examples. The commands appear benign and related to the advertised purpose, but users should only run them in trusted environments.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The CLI commands are directly present and intended to be run by an assistant. They are Chronicle-specific examples, so the risk is operational rather than malicious.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as Ruby Execution
low_findings.description: The static Ruby backtick findings are Markdown code spans and fenced examples. No Ruby code or command substitution syntax is present in the skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed context shows Markdown examples, not executable Ruby. The file is a single SKILL.md document with no script file or runtime wrapper.

low_findings.title: Home Directory Paths Are Documentation
low_findings.description: The hidden home directory paths describe Chronicle storage locations. They do not instruct the assistant to read or copy those files directly.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The paths appear under a current state reference section. There is no command that reads the database, transcripts, or configuration file directly.

low_findings.title: Sensitive and Blocker Patterns Are Textual False Positives
low_findings.description: The Windows SAM, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance alerts are caused by ordinary prose or tool names. No evidence of credential theft, cryptographic misuse, or host reconnaissance was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are descriptive text such as the skill description, an example saying SAME issue, a mistakes heading, and an MCP server note. No malicious semantic context is present.

content.user_title: Guide AI Assistants Through Chronicle Workflows
content.value_statement: AI assistants often repeat work when they miss prior project history. This skill gives search-first Chronicle guidance for MCP and CLI workflows.
actual_capabilities: Explains when to search Chronicle before starting implementation or debugging.
Shows equivalent MCP and CLI workflows for common Chronicle tasks.
Lists Chronicle tools for sessions, commits, milestones, roadmaps, and statistics.
Provides workflow patterns for new tasks, debugging, and project history review.
Documents current Chronicle storage concepts and session organization commands.
limitations: Requires Chronicle to be installed or available through an MCP server.
Does not provide Chronicle itself or configure the MCP server.
May surface sensitive local history if searches are too broad.
Provides guidance only; assistant behavior still depends on tool access.

use_cases.title: Start Work With Prior Context
use_cases.description: Search Chronicle before implementing a feature to find related sessions, decisions, and known failed approaches.

use_cases.title: Debug With Historical Evidence
use_cases.description: Look up previous errors, symptoms, and fixes before spending time on repeated diagnosis.

use_cases.title: Review Roadmaps and Milestones
use_cases.description: Use Chronicle roadmap and milestone guidance to connect current work with tracked next steps.

prompt_templates.title: Search Before Starting
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner workflow for a new task.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Chronicle to search for prior work related to this task. Summarize useful context before you implement anything.

prompt_templates.title: Check Roadmap Context
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the Chronicle roadmap and pending next steps for this repository. Tell me which items relate to my request.

prompt_templates.title: Investigate a Repeated Issue
prompt_templates.prompt: Search Chronicle for this error or symptom. Compare prior fixes, explain what changed, and recommend the next diagnostic step.

prompt_templates.title: Use MCP With CLI Fallback
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Chronicle MCP tools if available. If they are unavailable, use the Chronicle CLI and keep commands scoped to this project.

output_examples.input: Search Chronicle before implementing authentication changes.
output_examples.output: The assistant reports related prior sessions, notes a past failed approach, and identifies the most relevant roadmap item.

output_examples.input: Investigate a repeated MCP response size issue.
output_examples.output: The assistant finds the earlier session that solved the same problem and summarizes the previous fix before changing code.
The assistant summarizes active milestones, pending next steps, and any items that overlap with the requested task.

best_practices: Search Chronicle before implementation, debugging, or changing documented workflows.
Prefer MCP tools when available, and use CLI commands as a fallback.
Scope searches by project, topic, and date when sensitive history may exist.
anti_patterns: Do not skip Chronicle search when the task may repeat prior work.
Do not run broad history searches when a project-specific query is enough.
Do not update milestones or next steps without confirming the current task context.

faq.question: What is this skill for?
faq.answer: It guides AI assistants to use Chronicle history, roadmaps, sessions, and CLI or MCP tools effectively.

faq.question: Does it install Chronicle?
faq.answer: No. It assumes Chronicle is already installed or available through an MCP server.

faq.question: Can it work without MCP?
faq.answer: Yes. The guide includes CLI fallback workflows for environments without Chronicle MCP tools.

faq.question: What should users watch for?
faq.answer: Chronicle may contain local session history. Users should keep searches scoped when private project context exists.

faq.question: Does it execute commands automatically?
faq.answer: No. The skill is a Markdown guide. Assistants may follow its command examples when tool access allows it.

faq.question: Which tools are supported?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
